metadata.title: Automate workflows with hooks - Claude Code Docs
metadata.description: Run shell commands automatically when Claude Code edits files, finishes tasks, or needs input. Format code, send notifications, validate commands, and enforce project rules.

links.text: Get notified when Claude needs input
Get notified when Claude needs input
Reload environment when directory or files change
Defer a tool call for later

code_blocks.code:
#!/bin/bash
# protect-files.sh

INPUT=$(cat)
FILE_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty')

PROTECTED_PATTERNS=(".env" "package-lock.json" ".git/")

for pattern in "${PROTECTED_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
  if [[ "$FILE_PATH" == *"$pattern"* ]]; then
    echo "Blocked: $FILE_PATH matches protected pattern '$pattern'" >&2
    exit 2
  fi
done

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
INPUT=$(cat)
COMMAND=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command')

if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -q "drop table"; then
  echo "Blocked: dropping tables is not allowed" >&2  # stderr becomes Claude's feedback
  exit 2                                               # exit 2 = block the action
fi

exit 0  # exit 0 = let it proceed

echo '{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"ls"}}' | ./my-hook.sh
echo $?  # Check the exit code

#!/bin/bash
INPUT=$(cat)
if [ "$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.stop_hook_active')" = "true" ]; then
  exit 0  # Allow Claude to stop
fi
# ... rest of your hook logic

# In ~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc
if [[ $- == *i* ]]; then
  echo "Shell ready"
fi
